source_title: A dossier of data journalism teaching strategies
author: DataJournalism.com

extracts.extract: Thinking about data 'from the beginning reinforces for students that it is a core skill, just like interviewing or checking facts'.
extracts.chapter: Australia — Dr Kayt Davies, Edith Cowan University
extracts.relevance_rationale: Treating data as a core journalism skill alongside interviewing and fact-checking is a durable pedagogical principle

extracts.extract: Remember that the data is never the story. Many of the most powerful stories come from weaving together findings with stories of individuals and communities.
extracts.chapter: United States — Jeff Kelly Lowenstein, Grand Valley State University
extracts.relevance_rationale: The principle that data alone is never the story and must be woven with human narratives is timeless editorial wisdom

extracts.extract: IRE Contest entries are your friend — Reading these teaches students how to reverse engineer an investigation.
extracts.chapter: United States — Jeff Kelly Lowenstein, Grand Valley State University
extracts.relevance_rationale: Learning to reverse-engineer investigations from exemplary work remains a valuable pedagogical method regardless of tools

extracts.extract: Build incrementally — Start with smaller stories and projects that become the basis for larger, more ambitious ones.
extracts.chapter: United States — Jeff Kelly Lowenstein, Grand Valley State University
extracts.relevance_rationale: Incremental scaffolding in data journalism education is a sound pedagogical approach independent of technology
